understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to progress through all levels of the KA59 puzzle by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile simultaneously. Each level had a finite step budget, and winning a level required all '+' goal tiles to be covered by selectable pieces at the same time, which in turn would progress and eventually complete the entire game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used pixel coordinates on a grid where each cell was 3 pixels. Actions attempted to shift the currently selected piece. Some objects were pushable blocks that would slide along with the piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls and boundaries could block movement in various ways, and the 'movement.{direction}_blocked' hint was a heuristic that didn't always mean an action would fail. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; the CLICK action with a pixel-coordinate target switched control to another selectable piece. Pieces and objects could interact with the environment to navigate around obstacles and position all selectables onto goals to complete a level.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to progress through all levels of the KA59 puzzle by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile simultaneously. Each level had a finite step budget, and winning a level required all '+' goal tiles to be covered by selectable pieces at the same time, which in turn would progress and eventually complete the entire game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used pixel coordinates on a grid where each cell was 3 pixels. Actions attempted to shift the currently selected piece. Some objects were pushable blocks that would slide along with the piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls and boundaries could block movement in various ways, and the 'movement.{direction}_blocked' hint was a heuristic that didn't always mean an action would fail. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; the CLICK action with a pixel-coordinate target switched control to another selectable piece. Pieces and objects could interact with the environment to navigate around obstacles and position all selectables onto goals to complete a level.
